LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FS SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYMENT, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AS TO PLAINTIFF JOHN RODIE. DEFENDANT FS SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYMENT, INC. DBA FOUR SEASONS HOTEL SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AS TO PLAINTIFF JOHN RODIE IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 21, 2020 AT 9:30 A.M. DEFENDANT FAILED IN ITS MOVING PAPERS TO AUTHENTICATE THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT, AND DID NOT FILE AND SERVE THE DECLARATION OF JIMMIE LOPEZ UNTIL JANUARY 22, 2020, THE DAY ON WHICH PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION WAS DUE. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE ANY FURTHER OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION BY FEBRUARY 11, 2020. (SEE JAY V. MAHAFFEY (2013) 218 CAL.APP.4TH 1522, 1538 [IF ADDITIONAL EVIDENTIARY MATTER INCLUDED WITH THE REPLY IS PERMITTED, THE OTHER PARTY SHOULD BE GIVEN THE OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND].) NO FURTHER REPLY WILL BE ACCEPTED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2020 Set For Trial On Dec-07-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Feb-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Hayden R. Pace, Esq. of INTERNATIONAL SOCIETY OF HOSPITALITY CONSULTANTS, T:415.943.9471, for defendant/moving party;
Bryan McCormack, Esq. of MCCORMACK & ERLICH, T:415.296.8420, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT FS SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYMENT, INC. DBA FOUR SEASON HOTEL SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS as follows:

Defendant FS San Francisco Employment, Inc. dba Four Seasons Hotel San Francisco's motion to stay case pending arbitration is denied in light of the court's ruling denying the motion to compel arbitration.

Counsel for the plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration As To John Rodie
Off Calendar Feb-21-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Hayden R. Pace, Esq. of INTERNATIONAL SOCIET OF HOSPITALITY CONSULTANTS, T:415.943.9471, for defendant/moving party;
Bryan McCormack, Esq. of MCCORMACK & ERLICH, T:415.296.8420, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was contested and argued. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT FS SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYMENT, INC. DBA FOUR SEASONS HOTEL SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Arbitration As To John Rodie as follows:

Defendant FS San Francisco Employment, Inc. dba Four Seasons Hotel San Francisco's motion to compel arbitration as to plaintiff John Rodie is denied.

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Continued To Feb-21-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FS SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYMENT, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY CASE PENDING ARBITRATION AS TO PLAINTIFF JOHN RODIE.

Defendant FS San Francisco Employment, Inc. dba Four Seasons Hotel San Francisco's motion to stay case pending arbitration as to plaintiff John Rodie is continued to February 21, 2020 at 9:30 a.m.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration As To John Rodie
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Continued To Feb-21-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FS SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYMENT, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration As To Plaintiff John Rodie.

Defendant FS San Francisco Employment, Inc. dba Four Seasons Hotel San Francisco's motion to compel arbitration as to plaintiff John Rodie is continued to February 21, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. Defendant failed in its moving papers to authenticate the arbitration agreement, and did not file and serve the Declaration of Jimmie Lopez until January 22, 2020, the day on which plaintiff's opposition to the motion was due. Plaintiff may file any further opposition to the motion by February 11, 2020. (See Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1538 [if additional evidentiary matter included with the reply is permitted, the other party should be given the opportunity to respond].) No further reply will be accepted.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
